inclusion_criteria: Human studies conducted in Southeast Asia (ASEAN: Vietnam, Cambodia, Lao PDR, Thailand, Myanmar, Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, Philippines, Brunei, and Timor-Leste/East Timor) or reporting extractable data for participants from these countries

Include an exposure to agricultural land-use (living or working in agricultural areas such as farms/plantations/agroforestry/aquaculture) and a comparator group unexposed to agricultural land-use (non-agricultural residents/workers) within the study

Report infection prevalence (e.g., point/period prevalence, seroprevalence, parasitological or PCR positivity) for human infectious diseases, including hookworm, malaria, scrub typhus, spotted fever group rickettsioses, or other pathogen infections, with data by exposure group or a prevalence-based effect estimate
Observational human study designs with extractable data (cross-sectional, cohort, or case-control)
exclusion_criteria: Not conducted in the ASEAN region and does not provide separable data for ASEAN participants
No agricultural exposure contrast or no unexposed comparator (e.g., agricultural-only samples, purely ecological designs without individual-level exposure)
Outcomes do not report infection prevalence or related prevalence measures in humans (e.g., incidence-only, mortality-only, symptoms without diagnostic confirmation, vector/reservoir infection only)
Non-original or ineligible designs: laboratory-only, modeling-only, reviews, editorials, conference abstracts without full data, or duplicate datasets
screening_criteria.description: Select human observational studies from ASEAN countries that compare infection prevalence between individuals exposed to agricultural land-use and unexposed individuals.

included_articles.title: Rickettsial seropositivity in the indigenous community and animal farm workers, and vector surveillance in Peninsular Malaysia.
Socioeconomic and behavioural determinants of malaria among the migrants in gold mining, rubber and oil palm plantation areas in Myanmar.
